# MDE 1.5.0

## Features

- Added drag-and-drop opening for local Markdown files and folders.
- Workspace-internal Markdown drops open in the current editor and reveal the file in the Explorer.
- Workspace-internal folder drops refresh and select the folder in the current Explorer.
- External Markdown files and folders dropped onto an active workspace open in a new MDE window, while drops onto an empty window open in the current window.
- Added a non-blocking drop target overlay so users can see when MDE is ready to open a dropped resource.

## Bug Fixes

- Kept the Explorer theme selector padding visually even on all four sides while preserving the standard footer button height.
- Preserved manual folder collapse behavior after MDE auto-reveals the currently selected file in the Explorer.
- Unsupported dropped files now show a localized friendly error instead of changing the current workspace state.
- Canonicalized dropped-resource E2E paths so CI macOS `/var` and `/private/var` aliases do not misclassify workspace-internal drops.

## Breaking Changes

- None.

## Maintenance

- Added path inspection IPC support so the renderer can distinguish Markdown files, folders, unsupported files, and other paths before choosing an open strategy.
- Added focused unit, integration, and E2E coverage for dropped resources, Explorer refresh behavior, and footer theme control sizing.

## Documentation

- Updated the Chinese user manual with the new drag-and-drop opening behavior for workspaces, Markdown files, external resources, and unsupported files.

## Verification

- `npm run lint`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm run test:unit`
- `npm run test:integration`
- `npm run docs:build`
- `npm run test:e2e`
- `npx playwright test tests/e2e/markdown-editor.e2e.test.ts -g "opens a dropped workspace Markdown file in the current window"`

## Artifacts

- macOS DMG and ZIP artifacts are produced by the GitHub Release workflow. If signing secrets are not configured, macOS artifacts are unsigned.
- Windows NSIS installer and updater artifacts are produced by the GitHub Release workflow.
